verdict: PASS — all hard checks clear; 1 minor soft finding (abstract-first ordering in individual measure subsections, below escalation threshold); no scope creep, no forward refs, no assessment references, Khan Academy cited, length within target.

format.notes: No frontmatter; sidecar parses cleanly; all six sections present in correct order.

length.notes: 1263 words (200 wpm → 6.3 min). Both within target.

citations.notes: Khan Academy [1] cited at lines 15, 22, 29, 44, 79 and more. All inline markers have bibliography entries; no orphans.

forward_references.notes: All terms used ('stochastic', 'temperature', 'probability distribution', 'tokens', 'deterministic') are from the permitted prior-concept list (7.1–7.5). No concepts beyond 7.6 introduced.

sampled_assertions.assertion: Mean = sum divided by count; 10-score example (1,1,0,1,1,0,1,0,1,1) → mean 0.7
Median sorted scores: two middle values (pos 5+6) both 1 → median 1
Mode text example: Yes 6, No 3, Partially 1 → mode 'Yes'
Temperature table: low → strong mode/stable mean; high → weak mode/variable mean
Strong mode at low temperature signals near-deterministic behaviour
Mode works on text categories where mean/median do not apply
Run at least 10 trials before drawing conclusions
Reporting only the mean hides problems; all three measures should appear together
Mean close to median + strong mode = signal of consistent AI system

faithfulness.notes: All 10 sampled assertions trace directly to corpus sections.

findings.detail: Individual measure subsections (e.g. '### Mean — the overall average') present the formal definition before the example. The Key Concepts lead paragraph uses a lens analogy first, partially mitigating this. Isolated to three subsection headers; overall reading is concrete and well-paced.

beginner_readability.notes: 1 soft finding (abstract-first on subsection level). 'Stochastic' and 'deterministic' reused from prior topics (7.4) — no redefinition required. All other terms defined on first use with bold-term format.

formatting.notes: Paragraphs consistently 2–4 sentences. Worked Example uses numbered steps. In Practice uses bullet lists. Tables used for comparisons. Inline examples appear alongside concept introductions in Key Concepts.

assessment_reference.notes: No assessment IDs, due-week references, or grade weights appear in the reading. (Corpus had A3 references; these were correctly excluded from the reading.)

scope.notes: Reading stays entirely within mean/median/mode. No variance, std-dev, or IQR taught. 'Red-teaming', 'adversarial', and 'benchmark' (corpus soft findings) are absent from the reading.

escalation_note: 1 soft finding (abstract_first) is below the 3-finding escalation threshold and no scope_creep was found; no hard-fail escalation triggered.
gold_example_note: No gold example found in .claude/references/gold-examples/reading/ — assessment made against written standards in artifact-mix.json and SKILL.md.
